pattern: Extending any subarray that already appears nowhere else by one more element on either side keeps it appearing nowhere else, so the lengths where a one-of-a-kind subarray exists form an unbroken run up to the full array; binary search on the length, and hash each window to test it quickly.
hints: If a subarray of some length never repeats anywhere else, stretching it by one more element (in either direction) still never repeats — so once a length works, every longer length works too, all the way up to the whole array.
That one-way property means you can binary search on the candidate length instead of checking every length from 1 upward.
To test a single candidate length quickly, hash every window of that length (a rolling hash lets you slide the window in O(1) per step) and check whether any hash value occurs exactly once.

edge_inputs.description: a single element, trivially unique on its own
two equal elements, only the full array is unique
all elements already distinct, length one suffices
a palindrome-like run where short windows repeat but a longer one does not
one rare value breaking up an otherwise fully repeating pattern

stress.description: a long run of one repeated value forcing the answer toward the full array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    v = rng.randint(1, 5)
    nums = [v] * n
    return [nums]

stress.description: random values across the full allowed range and length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: a small alphabet repeated densely, forcing many hash collisions to resolve
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 3) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
